definition: Staggered first-round lags across sectors can create an inflation breadth signal that mimics genuine second-round effects, potentially trapping central banks into over-tightening. A plausible but unestablished hypothesis — fitting a 5-stage model to 2-3 observations exceeds degrees of freedom. Current evidence cannot distinguish lagged first-round passthrough mimicking breadth from genuine second-round broadening.
evidence: ECB 2023 Economic Bulletin cited as supporting evidence
2021-23 sequence (goods → services → shelter) consistent with either interpretation
Challenger_02 overfitting critique applies: 5-stage model from 2-3 observations
Pair_3 debate status: plausible hypothesis (~5/10), not established mechanism
open_questions: Can sector-level leading indicators distinguish genuine second-round effects from lagged first-round?
Does this hypothesis imply central banks systematically over-tighten during commodity shocks?
What cross-country evidence would help adjudicate?
